Rapnet provides access to $9.4 billion in daily listings — instantly searchable across over 1.6 million diamonds, gemstones, and jewelry items. In 2025 Rapnet added a service for trading coloured gemstones in collaboration with the American Gem Trade Association (AGTA) — expanding the platform beyond diamonds into a comprehensive B2B marketplace for the entire jewelry trade. Uniglo Diamonds contributes certified Antwerp-manufactured natural and lab grown diamond inventory to this supply — representing the European manufacturer segment within Rapnet's global supplier network.

sections.heading: Uniglo Diamonds — A Verified Rapnet Diamond Supplier in Antwerp
paragraphs: Uniglo Diamonds is verified and listed on Rapnet as a certified natural and lab grown diamond supplier. We are based at Hoveniersstraat 30 in Antwerp's diamond district and have been operating from this address for over 30 years — making us one of the longest-established Antwerp-based suppliers accessible through the Rapnet platform.
How to Find Uniglo Diamonds on the Rapnet Platform
Finding Uniglo Diamonds within Rapnet is straightforward for existing platform members.
bullets1: Search by supplier name. Log in to your Rapnet account and use the member directory search to find Uniglo Diamonds. Our verified supplier account and current certified diamond inventory are accessible within the platform directly.

Search by Antwerp origin. Use Rapnet's advanced search filters to filter inventory by supplier location — selecting Antwerp or Belgium as the origin — to identify Uniglo Diamonds listings within the broader platform inventory. Combined with your preferred certification filter (IGI, GIA, or HRD), this narrows the search to our specific certified stock efficiently.

Use the Buy Request feature. Post a Buy Request and sellers who have that diamond will contact you. For specific shapes, grades, or carat weights you are seeking, posting a Buy Request that includes Antwerp or Uniglo Diamonds as preferred source criteria will connect you directly with our team for that specification.

Contact Uniglo directly to establish a preferred supplier relationship. Rapnet allows trade buyers to build preferred supplier relationships within the platform. Contact our Antwerp team at suraj@uniglodiamonds.com or +32 03 500 91 07 to discuss establishing a preferred supply arrangement both through Rapnet and as a direct wholesale account.
paragraphs2: What Makes Uniglo One of Rapnet's Strongest Certified Diamond Suppliers

Trade with confidence and security knowing that the people you deal with in the Rapnet community are trade verified by Rapaport. Within that verified community, Uniglo Diamonds stands apart from most other Rapnet suppliers for specific, demonstrable reasons.
bullets2: Direct Antwerp manufacturer — not a reseller. Most suppliers on Rapnet are resellers, distributors, or trading companies purchasing from manufacturers and listing on the platform for a margin. Uniglo Diamonds manufactures in our own Antwerp facility — giving Rapnet buyers who source from us direct manufacturer pricing with no intermediary markup. For a platform where pricing is assessed relative to the Rapaport benchmark, sourcing from a direct manufacturer consistently delivers better value per carat than sourcing from reseller suppliers listing equivalent certified goods.

Tri-certification: GIA, IGI, and HRD. We list stones on Rapnet certified by all three globally recognised grading authorities. Rapnet's vetting process ensures only trade-verified professionals list on the platform — and our tri-certification capability gives Rapnet buyers the flexibility to specify their preferred certification standard for any order.

30+ years of Antwerp diamond manufacturing heritage. Our manufacturing and quality assessment capabilities were developed over three decades in Antwerp's natural diamond trade. Every diamond we list on Rapnet reflects those standards — applied consistently to both natural and lab grown inventory.

Both natural and lab grown certified supply. Uniglo Diamonds lists both certified natural diamonds and certified lab grown diamonds on Rapnet — making us one of the few Antwerp-based platform suppliers who can serve Rapnet buyers across both product categories from a single, direct manufacturer relationship.
paragraphs3: Why Sourcing From an Antwerp Manufacturer on Rapnet Matters

Rapnet's platform gives trade buyers access to verified suppliers from across the global diamond trade — India, Israel, USA, Belgium, and beyond. Within that global network, the location and manufacturing credentials of individual suppliers matter for quality, accountability, and supply chain transparency.

Antwerp processes approximately 84% of the world's rough diamonds. The city's cutting and polishing standards, its HRD grading infrastructure, and its five centuries of diamond manufacturing expertise make Antwerp-sourced certified diamonds the most accountable and quality-assured supply available anywhere on the Rapnet platform. An Antwerp manufacturer listed on Rapnet is not simply another verified member — it is a supplier backed by the world's most rigorous diamond trade institutional framework.

sections.heading: What Diamonds Does Uniglo List on Rapnet?
paragraphs: Natural Diamonds Available Through Uniglo on Rapnet

Uniglo Diamonds lists certified natural diamonds on Rapnet across all major shapes — round brilliant, oval, cushion, pear, princess, emerald, asscher, marquise, radiant, and heart. Our natural diamond inventory on Rapnet covers the full commercial carat range from 0.30 carat through to premium large stones, across the full colour and clarity spectrum from D Flawless investment-grade specifications through to commercial-grade SI goods for mainstream jewelry retail applications.

Every natural diamond listed by Uniglo Diamonds on Rapnet carries a current grading report from GIA, IGI, or HRD — independently verifiable on the respective laboratory website using the certificate report number. Our natural diamond supply reflects the full heritage of Uniglo's 30+ year Antwerp manufacturing operation — cutting and polishing standards applied in Antwerp's diamond district, graded by internationally recognised institutions.
Lab Grown Diamonds Available Through Uniglo on Rapnet

Uniglo Diamonds lists certified lab grown diamonds on Rapnet across all major shapes and across the full commercial carat range — from 0.30 carat melee through to 5+ carat premium solitaires. Colour grades span D through J for colorless and near-colorless applications. Clarity grades span IF through SI. Both CVD and HPHT manufacturing methods are available with method explicitly identified on every certificate and listing.

For Rapnet buyers who are expanding their lab grown diamond sourcing — or who are evaluating which Rapnet supplier to prioritise for certified lab grown inventory — Uniglo Diamonds offers the combination of direct Antwerp manufacturer pricing, tri-certification capability, and 30+ year industry heritage that distinguishes us from most lab grown suppliers listed on the platform.
Certifications Available on Uniglo's Rapnet Listings

Every diamond Uniglo Diamonds lists on Rapnet carries a current grading report from GIA, IGI, or HRD. Certificate numbers are listed within our Rapnet inventory entries and are independently verifiable on the respective laboratory websites before any purchase is confirmed. Laser inscriptions — the grading report number inscribed on the diamond's girdle, visible under 10x magnification — are confirmed on every stone, providing physical verification that each stone matches its certificate.

For the European and global trade market served by Rapnet, GIA certification carries the highest global prestige and is particularly valued for premium natural diamond transactions. IGI certification is the most widely used standard for lab grown diamonds globally and is well-recognised across all major Rapnet buyer markets. HRD certification carries particular authority in the Belgian and European professional trade — and as an Antwerp-based manufacturer, Uniglo Diamonds has a direct relationship with HRD that adds an additional layer of institutional credibility for European Rapnet buyers.
Pricing on Rapnet — How Uniglo's Inventory Is Priced

The Rapnet Price List provides market-driven, up-to-the-minute pricing for diamonds based on real transaction data on Rapnet, reflecting actual trading conditions. Uniglo Diamonds lists certified diamonds on Rapnet at transparent, competitive pricing relative to the Rapaport Price List benchmark — giving buyers the pricing reference clarity needed to assess our inventory efficiently against the global market standard.

Rapnet allows members to negotiate and trade directly with no commission or transaction fees. This means the price you see for Uniglo Diamonds inventory on Rapnet reflects our direct manufacturer wholesale pricing — without platform commission layers on top of the listed price. For Rapnet buyers evaluating Rapaport discount percentages across comparable certified inventory, Uniglo's manufacturer-level pricing consistently positions our listings at competitive discount levels relative to the Rapaport benchmark.

sections.heading: Rapnet Diamond Pricing Tools — What Sourcing Jewelers Need to Know
paragraphs: What Is the Rapaport Price List and How Is It Used?

The Rapaport Price List is a benchmark pricing guide widely used in the diamond industry to establish diamond prices, published weekly at midnight on Thursday EST. It provides a standardised reference for diamond traders, jewelers, and wholesalers to determine the market value of diamonds.

In practice, diamond prices on Rapnet are expressed as a percentage discount or premium to the Rapaport Price List — for example, a diamond listed at "Rapaport minus 40%" means the asking price is 40% below the relevant Rapaport Price List entry for that specification. Understanding Rapaport discounts allows Rapnet buyers to compare pricing across different suppliers for identical specifications — the most efficient way to evaluate whether a specific listing is competitively priced against the global market.
What Is the Rapnet Price List vs the Rapaport Price List?

The Rapaport Price List is a weekly benchmark updated by the Rapaport Group. The Rapnet Price List is an online, real-time pricing guide accessible exclusively to Rapnet members — providing market-driven, up-to-the-minute pricing based on real transaction data on Rapnet. Unlike the Rapaport Price List, the Rapnet Price List reflects more dynamic, actual trading conditions — making it a useful tool for buyers and sellers to gauge realistic diamond prices during negotiations.

For jewelers sourcing through Rapnet, the Rapnet Price List is the more operationally useful pricing tool — it reflects what diamonds are actually trading at in real time on the platform, rather than the weekly benchmark that may lag actual market movements. Using both tools together gives the most complete picture of current market pricing for any diamond specification.
Does Rapnet Charge Commission or Transaction Fees?

Rapnet allows members to negotiate and trade directly with diamond suppliers with no commission or transaction fees. The platform operates on a membership subscription model — buyers and sellers pay a membership fee for access, but individual transactions carry no platform commission. This is a significant practical advantage over some competing platforms where per-transaction fees erode the margin advantage of wholesale pricing. For high-volume Rapnet buyers, the absence of transaction fees means the full pricing advantage of sourcing from a direct manufacturer like Uniglo Diamonds flows directly to your bottom line.
Rapnet Financing — What Options Are Available?

Rapnet offers immediate and flexible financing — financing from partners at Del Gatto lets buyers use their inventory as collateral to get capital when buying Rapnet diamonds. This financing option is available directly within the Rapnet platform and is particularly valuable for jewelry businesses managing the balance between inventory investment and working capital.

For buyers who want financing arrangements directly with Uniglo Diamonds — outside the Rapnet platform — we offer our own flexible wholesale financing for qualified accounts. Contact our Antwerp team to discuss direct financing terms for large volume or custom orders placed outside the platform.

sections.heading: Sourcing Certified Diamonds Through Rapnet — The Complete Process
paragraphs: Step 1 — Apply for and Receive Rapnet Membership

New applicants can apply online or contact Rapnet directly. Applicants need to provide a business licence with full contact details, business references, government-issued documentation showing current legal registration, and sufficient proof that the business is operating in the diamond or jewelry trade and is in good standing. Rapnet's vetting process typically takes a few business days for established trade professionals with proper documentation.
Step 2 — Find Uniglo Diamonds on the Rapnet Platform

Once your Rapnet membership is active, search for Uniglo Diamonds in the member directory or filter the inventory search by Antwerp or Belgium origin with IGI, GIA, or HRD certification selected. Our current certified inventory of natural and lab grown diamonds is listed within the platform under the Uniglo Diamonds supplier account. For specific sourcing requirements — matched parcels, custom specifications, or large volume orders — contact our Antwerp team directly before placing the Rapnet order.
Step 3 — Trade Directly With No Commission Fees

Negotiate and trade directly with no commissions or listing and transaction fees. Place your order through Rapnet's trading infrastructure or use the platform's messaging system to communicate directly with our Antwerp team about specific requirements before confirming any purchase. For custom or large volume orders, direct communication with Uniglo Diamonds outside the platform is available alongside your Rapnet account.
Step 4 — Receive Certified Diamonds With Full Documentation

Every order placed through Rapnet from Uniglo Diamonds arrives with a current grading report from GIA, IGI, or HRD — the certificate number verifiable independently on the respective laboratory website. Every stone has a confirmed laser inscription on the girdle matching the certificate number. Full supply chain documentation confirming natural or lab grown origin is included with every shipment.

sections.heading: Rapnet vs Ordering Direct From Uniglo Diamonds — What Is the Difference?
paragraphs: When to Source Through the Rapnet Platform
bullets1: Sourcing through Rapnet works best when you want the platform's established trade infrastructure — including access to the Rapaport Price List benchmark, no commission or transaction fees, and the security of trading within a Rapaport-verified buyer and seller community.
When you want to use Rapnet's Buy Request feature to source specific stones across multiple suppliers simultaneously.
When you are pricing diamonds relative to the Rapaport benchmark and want the full context of the global market pricing environment.
And when the credibility of trading within the world's most established and longest-running diamond marketplace is important to your business.
paragraphs2: When to Order Directly From Uniglo Diamonds in Antwerp
bullets2: Ordering directly from Uniglo Diamonds is the stronger option when you need matched parcels for manufacturing applications — precise grade consistency across a full set of stones for tennis bracelets, eternity rings, or pavé settings.
When you are placing large volume orders where direct manufacturer pricing offers a further advantage over platform-mediated pricing.
When you have custom cutting or specification requirements that require direct manufacturing communication.
When you want to visit our Antwerp showroom and inspect certified stones in person.
And when you want a direct supply relationship with full manufacturer accountability that does not depend on any third-party platform.
paragraphs3: How to Use Both Rapnet and Direct Ordering Together

The most commercially effective approach for many trade buyers is using both channels in parallel — sourcing standard certified inventory through Rapnet for platform pricing transparency and trade infrastructure, while maintaining a direct account with Uniglo Diamonds for custom orders, matched parcels, volume purchasing, and showroom visits. Our Antwerp team actively supports buyers using this dual-channel approach and can advise on which route best serves each specific order requirement.

sections.heading: Why Uniglo Diamonds Is the Best Antwerp Supplier to Source From on Rapnet
paragraphs: Direct Manufacturer — Not a Reseller or Broker

Uniglo Diamonds manufactures certified natural and lab grown diamonds in our own Antwerp facility. We do not purchase from third-party manufacturers and relist on Rapnet for a margin. Every stone Uniglo lists on Rapnet is priced at direct manufacturer level — the lowest price tier in the supply chain — with full manufacturing accountability from the company that actually produced it.
30+ Years of Antwerp Diamond Manufacturing Heritage

Uniglo Diamonds has operated from Hoveniersstraat 30 in Antwerp's diamond district for over three decades — a verifiable, 30-year business history at the centre of the world's most established diamond manufacturing infrastructure. Within that independent platform, Uniglo Diamonds represents one of the longest-established Antwerp manufacturer accounts — bringing a depth of industry standing and quality heritage that newer platform entrants cannot match.
Tri-Certification — GIA, IGI and HRD on Every Stone

Uniglo Diamonds lists certified diamonds on Rapnet from all three globally recognised grading authorities — GIA, IGI, and HRD. For Rapnet buyers who sell into multiple markets with different certification preferences, our tri-certification capability means a single Antwerp manufacturer account covers all their certification requirements across both natural and lab grown inventory categories.
Full Natural and Lab Grown Inventory on One Platform

Uniglo Diamonds is one of the very few Antwerp-based Rapnet suppliers who list both certified natural diamonds and certified lab grown diamonds in volume. For trade buyers building mixed inventory — carrying both natural and lab grown certified stock — a single Antwerp manufacturer relationship on Rapnet that covers both product categories simplifies sourcing, reduces supplier management overhead, and ensures consistent quality standards across the full diamond inventory.
Custom Orders and Matched Parcels Beyond the Platform

For jewelry manufacturers producing multi-stone pieces, Uniglo Diamonds provides matched parcel capability beyond what standard Rapnet platform ordering accommodates. Direct contact with our Antwerp team allows us to prepare precision-matched parcels — consistent colour, clarity, and cut grades across the full set — and confirm availability before the order is formally processed through the platform. This manufacturing-level service is one of the most commercially valuable capabilities Uniglo offers to Rapnet-connected trade buyers.

items.question: How does Rapnet work for jewelers sourcing diamonds?
items.answer: Rapnet gives access to over 1.8 million diamonds, coloured gems and jewelry — allowing members to buy and sell safely and easily at the best prices, directly from verified and trusted suppliers. items.question: What is the Rapaport Price List and how does it affect diamond prices?
items.answer: The Rapaport Price List is a benchmark pricing guide published weekly at midnight Thursday EST, providing a standardised reference for diamond traders, jewelers, and wholesalers to determine the market value of diamonds. Diamond prices on Rapnet are typically expressed as a percentage discount or premium to this benchmark — allowing buyers to compare pricing across suppliers for identical specifications. Uniglo Diamonds lists certified inventory at transparent, competitive Rapaport discount levels — giving Rapnet buyers immediate pricing clarity.

items.question: Does Rapnet sell certified lab grown diamonds?

items.answer: Rapnet allows members to negotiate and trade directly with diamond suppliers with no commission or transaction fees. labGrownDiamondsPage.metaDescription: Buy certified lab grown diamonds wholesale direct from Antwerp's trusted manufacturer. IGI, GIA & HRD certified. Wholesale pricing for jewelers, retailers & private buyers. Request pricing today.
labGrownDiamondsPage.pageHeading: Where to Buy Lab Grown Diamonds Wholesale — The Complete Guide for Jewelers and Buyers
labGrownDiamondsPage.introPara1: Uniglo Diamonds is a certified lab grown diamond wholesale supplier based in Antwerp, Belgium — the world's diamond capital. We supply IGI, GIA, and HRD certified loose lab grown diamonds directly to jewelers, retailers, brands, and private buyers across Europe and globally. With over 30 years of manufacturing experience and our own production facility in Antwerp, we eliminate the middleman and offer wholesale pricing on every stone.
labGrownDiamondsPage.introPara2: Whether you are a jeweler sourcing inventory, a brand building a new collection, or a private buyer who wants diamond quality at wholesale value — buying lab grown diamonds wholesale from a direct manufacturer in Antwerp is the smartest decision you can make in today's diamond market.
labGrownDiamondsPage.introPara3: This guide covers everything you need to know: where to buy, what to look for in a wholesale supplier, how certified lab grown diamonds are priced, what makes Antwerp the world's most trusted source, and how Uniglo Diamonds serves both business buyers and individual customers.

whatAreLabGrown.heading: What Are Lab Grown Diamonds and Why Is Wholesale Demand Exploding?
whatAreLabGrown.para1: Lab grown diamonds are real diamonds. They are not simulants, not cubic zirconia, and not glass. They are chemically, physically, and optically identical to natural mined diamonds — the only difference is where they were formed. A mined diamond forms over billions of years under the Earth's crust. A lab grown diamond replicates that exact process in a controlled environment over a period of weeks, using either Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD) or High-Pressure High-Temperature (HPHT) technology.
whatAreLabGrown.para2: The result is a diamond that shares the same crystal structure, the same hardness (10 on the Mohs scale), the same refractive index, and the same brilliance as its mined equivalent. Not even a trained gemologist can distinguish between the two with the naked eye. Only specialist equipment identifies the origin.
whatAreLabGrown.para3: The global market for lab grown diamonds was valued at approximately $29.73 billion in 2025, and is projected to reach $97.85 billion by 2034 — growing at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 14.15% (Precedence Research, 2025). This is not a trend. This is a permanent structural shift in the diamond industry.
whatAreLabGrown.para4: In Europe alone, the lab grown diamond market accounts for over 30% of global market share, valued at approximately $6.75 billion in 2024, with a projected CAGR of 9.1% through 2031 (Cognitive Market Research, 2025). Jewelers across Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, France, and the UK are rapidly expanding their lab grown diamond offerings to meet surging consumer demand — particularly from millennial and Gen Z buyers who prioritise sustainability, ethics, and value without compromising on quality.
whatAreLabGrown.para5: For wholesale buyers, this creates an urgent opportunity. The jewelers and retailers who build their lab grown diamond supply chains now, with a certified, reliable Antwerp-based manufacturer, are the ones who will dominate their markets over the next decade.

costSection.heading: How Much Do Lab Grown Diamonds Cost Wholesale?
costSection.para1: One of the most critical advantages of sourcing lab grown diamonds wholesale from a direct manufacturer like Uniglo Diamonds is price. By working directly with the manufacturer in Antwerp — rather than through a broker, reseller, or trading platform — buyers access true wholesale pricing with no intermediary markups.
costSection.para2: Here is the current market picture for lab grown diamond pricing in 2025–2026:
costSection.para3: A 1-carat lab grown diamond now retails at approximately $1,000 on average, compared to approximately $4,200 for an equivalent natural diamond — a price difference of over 75% (Rio Grande Guardian, March 2026). At the wholesale level, those margins extend further, allowing jewelers and retailers to achieve significantly stronger profit margins than traditional natural diamond inventory allows.
costSection.priceRangesHeading: Lab Grown Diamond Price Ranges by Carat (2025–2026 Market Data):
costSection.para5: Wholesale prices available directly through Uniglo Diamonds. Contact us for current pricing on specific grades, shapes and quantities.
costSection.para6: Lab grown diamond prices have declined significantly since 2020 due to rapid improvements in manufacturing efficiency and increased supply — making now the most financially advantageous time in history to build or expand a lab grown diamond inventory. Leading market analysts suggest prices are approaching their natural floor and may begin to stabilise from 2026 onward.
costSection.para7: For wholesale buyers purchasing in volume directly from a manufacturer, these price points translate directly into competitive advantage in the retail market — allowing you to offer better stones at better prices than competitors sourcing through traditional supply chains.

bestPlaceSection.heading: Where Is the Best Place to Buy Lab Grown Diamonds Wholesale?
bestPlaceSection.para1: This is the question every jeweler, brand buyer, and serious private buyer eventually asks. The answer depends on three factors: certification, origin, and direct access.
bestPlaceSection.certificationPara1: Never buy wholesale lab grown diamonds without a grading report from a globally recognised gemological laboratory. The three most trusted certifications in the European and global diamond market are:
certificationBullets: GIA (Gemological Institute of America) — The most globally recognised grading authority. GIA lab grown diamond reports cover the same 4Cs grading (Cut, Colour, Clarity, Carat) applied to natural diamonds. GIA certification is particularly valued in the US market and by high-end fine jewelers worldwide.

IGI (International Gemological Institute) — The world's largest independent gemological laboratory and the certification body most commonly associated with lab grown diamonds globally. IGI certification is widely accepted across Europe, Asia, and the Americas. The majority of lab grown diamonds in the wholesale market carry IGI certificates due to IGI's dedicated lab grown diamond grading infrastructure.

HRD (Hoge Raad voor Diamant — Diamond High Council) — Based in Antwerp, Belgium, HRD certification carries particular weight in the European market. For buyers sourcing from European suppliers, HRD certification is a strong trust signal.
bestPlaceSection.certificationPara2: At Uniglo Diamonds, every lab grown diamond in our inventory carries a current certificate from GIA, IGI, or HRD. We do not supply uncertified stones. This is non-negotiable for us because it should be non-negotiable for you.
bestPlaceSection.antwerpHeading: Why Antwerp Is the Right Source
bestPlaceSection.antwerpPara1: Antwerp processes approximately 84% of the world's rough diamonds. The city's Hoveniersstraat diamond district — where Uniglo Diamonds has been located for over three decades — is home to the world's most experienced diamond manufacturers, cutters, polishers and graders. Buying wholesale from Antwerp means buying from the origin of diamond expertise.
bestPlaceSection.antwerpPara2: This matters for wholesale buyers because:
antwerpBullets: The quality control standards applied in Antwerp are among the strictest in the world. Diamonds sourced through Antwerp manufacturers and certified by HRD, IGI, or GIA have been graded to internationally consistent standards — not to the variable standards sometimes applied in less regulated markets.

Supply chain transparency is embedded in Antwerp's culture. The city is the home of the Responsible Jewellery Council (RJC) and operates under the Kimberley Process framework. Every stone that leaves Antwerp is traceable. For wholesale buyers in regulated European and American markets, this matters enormously from a compliance and brand reputation perspective.
bestPlaceSection.directHeading: Why Direct From Manufacturer Beats Diamond Trading Platforms
bestPlaceSection.directPara1: Online diamond trading platforms and diamond brokers have their place in the market. But for serious wholesale buyers, there are significant limitations:
directBullets: Trading platforms aggregate inventory from multiple sellers. You rarely know who actually grew the diamond, where it was cut, or what quality control processes were applied.

Every intermediary in the supply chain adds margin to the final price you pay. A stone that moves from manufacturer to broker to trading platform to your purchase order has been marked up at each step.

Personalised supply relationships are impossible on trading platforms. If you need a specific shape, a specific clarity range, a custom order, or consistent supply at a known price — a direct manufacturer relationship is the only reliable solution.
bestPlaceSection.directPara2: Uniglo Diamonds offers direct manufacturer access at wholesale pricing, with the full backing of our Antwerp manufacturing facility, 30+ years of diamond expertise, and tri-certification from GIA, IGI, and HRD. There is no platform fee, no broker margin, and no uncertainty about origin.

whoShouldBuySection.heading: Who Should Buy Lab Grown Diamonds Wholesale?
whoShouldBuySection.jewelersPara1: If you operate a jewelry store, design studio, or online jewelry retail business, building your inventory around certified lab grown diamonds from a direct Antwerp manufacturer gives you several structural advantages in your market:
jewelersBullets: Higher margins on every sale. Because lab grown diamonds cost significantly less than mined alternatives at comparable grades, your retail markup on a lab grown diamond is typically more achievable without pricing yourself out of the market.

Larger stones, better grades, at accessible price points. Today's jewelry buyer — particularly the engagement ring buyer — is increasingly choosing lab grown diamonds because they can get a 2.5 carat stone for what they would have paid for a 1 carat mined stone five years ago. The average lab grown center diamond for an engagement ring grew from 1.31 carats in 2019 to 2.45 carats in 2025 — an 87% increase driven entirely by the affordability of lab grown stones (BriteCo, 2025). As a retailer, carrying large, high-clarity lab grown inventory positions you to serve this buyer.

A growing, loyal customer segment. Lab grown diamonds now account for over 47% of engagement ring center stones sold in 2025, up from just 6.3% in 2019 (BriteCo, 2025). This is no longer a niche. It is the mainstream. Retailers who do not offer lab grown diamonds at competitive price points are actively losing business to those who do.
whoShouldBuySection.brandsPara1: Brand buyers sourcing lab grown diamonds wholesale for new collections have particular requirements: consistency of supply, grading reliability, and the ability to customise specifications. At Uniglo Diamonds, we work directly with brands to source specific shapes, colours, clarity grades, and carat ranges to match collection requirements. Our manufacturing facility in Antwerp allows us to accommodate custom cutting and polishing requests for brand-specific designs.
whoShouldBuySection.privatePara1: You do not need to be a business to buy at wholesale. Uniglo Diamonds serves private buyers who want to purchase a certified lab grown diamond directly — either for a bespoke engagement ring, a personal jewelry commission, or as a considered purchase — at wholesale pricing rather than retail. This is one of the most significant advantages of buying directly from an Antwerp manufacturer. A private buyer who comes to Uniglo Diamonds for a 2-carat IGI-certified lab grown diamond is buying at a price that no high-street jeweler, no online retailer, and no trading platform can match, because they are buying from the source.

evaluateSupplierSection.heading: How to Evaluate a Lab Grown Diamond Wholesale Supplier
evaluateSupplierSection.para1: Not all wholesale suppliers are equal. Before committing to a wholesale supply relationship for lab grown diamonds, evaluate any supplier against these criteria:
bullets: Does every stone carry a current certificate from GIA, IGI, or HRD? This is the baseline. A wholesale supplier who cannot provide third-party grading reports for every stone they sell is not a supplier you should work with. Certification is not a formality — it is the buyer's guarantee that the grade claimed for the diamond is accurate, independent, and internationally recognised.

Is the supplier a direct manufacturer or a reseller? A direct manufacturer controls quality from the growing stage through to cutting, polishing, and grading. A reseller buys from manufacturers and sells to buyers — adding cost and removing accountability. Ask directly: do you manufacture your own diamonds, or do you source from third parties? At Uniglo Diamonds, we manufacture in our own Antwerp facility. The answer is unambiguous.

How long have they been in the diamond industry? Lab grown diamonds are a recent evolution in a very established industry. The most trustworthy wholesale suppliers are those who have deep roots in the natural diamond trade and have applied that expertise — in cutting, grading, and supply chain management — to lab grown stones. Uniglo Diamonds has been in the Antwerp diamond industry for over 30 years. Our lab grown diamond operation is built on decades of manufacturing expertise, not a startup pivot.

Can they offer flexible supply — shapes, sizes, grades, quantities? A quality wholesale supplier should be able to fulfil orders across all major shapes (round brilliant, oval, cushion, pear, princess, emerald, asscher, marquise, radiant, heart), all carat weights from 0.3 to 5+ carats, and across the full range of colour and clarity grades. They should also accommodate both small and large order quantities without imposing restrictive minimum order requirements that do not reflect your actual business needs.

Do they offer transparent, documented pricing? Wholesale pricing should be clear, consistent, and documented. A supplier who cannot provide firm pricing in writing, or whose prices fluctuate without explanation, is not a reliable wholesale partner.

cvdVsHptSection.heading: CVD vs HPHT — Which Lab Grown Diamond Manufacturing Method Should You Source?
cvdVsHptSection.para1: When sourcing lab grown diamonds wholesale, you will encounter two manufacturing methods. Understanding the difference helps you make better sourcing decisions and answer questions from your retail customers.
cvdVsHptSection.cvdPara: CVD (Chemical Vapor Deposition) is currently the dominant method in the lab grown diamond market, accounting for approximately 45% of global market share in 2024 (Fortune Business Insights, 2025). In the CVD process, a diamond seed is placed in a sealed chamber filled with carbon-rich gas. The gas is ionised into plasma, causing carbon atoms to deposit on the seed and grow layer by layer into a diamond crystal. CVD diamonds are valued for their exceptional purity, precise colour and clarity control, and the ability to grow very large stones. The majority of premium colorless lab grown diamonds in the wholesale market today are CVD.
cvdVsHptSection.hphtPara: HPHT (High-Pressure High-Temperature) replicates the geological conditions under which natural diamonds form — extreme pressure and extreme heat applied to a carbon source. HPHT diamonds are often characterised by slightly different crystal growth patterns and can exhibit subtle yellow or brown tints at lower grades. However, high-quality HPHT colorless diamonds are produced and are widely traded at the wholesale level. The HPHT method is also used to enhance or colour treat existing diamonds — a separate process distinct from primary HPHT diamond growing.
cvdVsHptSection.para4: At Uniglo Diamonds, we supply both CVD and HPHT certified lab grown diamonds. Our recommendation on method is always guided by your specific application — whether that is a high-clarity colorless solitaire for an engagement ring or a fancy coloured stone for a fashion piece.

whyUniglo.heading: Why Uniglo Diamonds Is the Right Wholesale Partner
whyUniglo.para1: We understand that choosing a wholesale diamond supplier is a significant business decision. Here is what sets Uniglo Diamonds apart from every alternative in the market:
bullets: 30+ years in Antwerp's diamond district. We are not new to this industry. Our roots are in Antwerp's Hoveniersstraat — the street that for generations has been the heartbeat of the global diamond trade. That heritage means our quality standards, our supply chain relationships, and our grading expertise are built on decades of real-world experience, not on a recently-acquired product line.

Own manufacturing facility. Unlike resellers and brokers, Uniglo Diamonds operates its own manufacturing facility in Antwerp. This means we control quality at every stage of the process. It also means we can accommodate custom orders, specific grade requirements, and flexible supply arrangements that purely trading-oriented businesses cannot.

Tri-certification: GIA, IGI, and HRD. We are one of the few wholesale suppliers in Europe who can supply lab grown diamonds certified by all three major grading authorities. This gives our wholesale buyers maximum flexibility — whether your market prefers GIA, your brand specification calls for IGI, or your European clientele expects HRD.

Free diamond estimation service. For buyers looking to understand the precise value of existing diamonds they hold — whether natural or lab grown — Uniglo Diamonds provides a complimentary, no-obligation diamond estimation service. This is particularly valuable for jewelers evaluating trade-in stock or purchasing second-hand diamond inventory.

Diamond financing options. We offer flexible financing arrangements for qualified wholesale buyers, allowing you to build inventory without the full capital requirement of outright purchase. This is a significant competitive advantage for growing jewelry businesses managing cash flow alongside inventory expansion.

Supply chain transparency and ethical sourcing. Every lab grown diamond we supply is produced without the ethical concerns associated with diamond mining — no forced labour, no environmental destruction, no conflict financing. Our supply chain documentation is complete and available on request. For buyers selling to ethically-conscious consumers — particularly in European markets where provenance increasingly matters — this is a material commercial advantage.

europeMarketSection.heading: The Lab Grown Diamond Market in Europe — What Wholesale Buyers Need to Know in 2026
europeMarketSection.para1: The European lab grown diamond market represents one of the most significant opportunities for jewelers and retailers on the continent. Europe accounts for over 30% of the global lab grown diamond market, with a market value of approximately $6.75 billion in 2024, projected to grow at a CAGR of 9.1% through 2031.
europeMarketSection.para2: Lab grown diamonds now account for over 45% of US engagement ring purchases by 2024 — and European markets are following closely behind, driven by strong consumer preference for ethical, sustainable luxury among millennial and Gen Z buyers who represent the primary engagement ring purchasing demographic.
europeMarketSection.para3: In 2019, only 6.3% of engagement rings had a lab grown center stone. By 2025, that figure had risen to 47.7% — a transformation that has no parallel in the history of the jewelry industry. The jewelers and brands who built their lab grown supply chains early are now the market leaders. The window to establish a position is still open — but it is narrowing.
europeMarketSection.para4: For wholesale buyers, the global lab grown diamond market is projected to expand from $29.73 billion in 2025 to approximately $97.85 billion by 2034 — representing a near tripling of market value over nine years. Positioning your business to serve this growing market, with a reliable, certified Antwerp wholesale supplier, is one of the highest-return strategic decisions available to a jewelry business today.

labGrownDiamondsPage.faqTitle: Frequently Asked Questions About Buying Lab Grown Diamonds Wholesale

faqs.question: What is the minimum order quantity for wholesale lab grown diamonds from Uniglo?
faqs.answer: We work with buyers of all sizes, from individual stones for private clients to bulk orders for retail chains. There is no fixed minimum order requirement that applies universally. Contact our wholesale team directly to discuss your specific requirements and we will provide pricing and availability accordingly.

faqs.question: Are lab grown diamonds real diamonds?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lab grown diamonds are 100% real diamonds. They have the same chemical composition (pure carbon in a cubic crystal structure), the same physical properties (hardness of 10 on the Mohs scale), and the same optical properties (brilliance, fire, scintillation) as natural mined diamonds. The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) in the United States officially updated its guidelines to confirm that lab grown diamonds are diamonds — the word 'synthetic' is no longer considered appropriate for marketing purposes because it implies they are imitation or fake, which they are not.

faqs.question: How do I know the grade of a lab grown diamond is accurate?
faqs.answer: Every lab grown diamond supplied by Uniglo Diamonds carries a grading report from GIA, IGI, or HRD — independent, internationally recognised gemological laboratories. These reports confirm the 4Cs (Cut, Colour, Clarity, Carat weight) and confirm the diamond's lab grown origin. The grading report is the buyer's guarantee that the grade assigned is accurate and independently verified.

faqs.question: Can jewelers buy lab grown diamonds directly from a manufacturer in Antwerp?
faqs.answer: Yes — and this is strongly advisable over purchasing through brokers or trading platforms. Buying directly from a manufacturer like Uniglo Diamonds gives you direct wholesale pricing, confirmed origin, direct quality accountability, and a supply relationship you can rely on for consistent future orders. Contact our B2B wholesale team to open an account and discuss supply terms.

faqs.question: What shapes of lab grown diamonds are available wholesale?
faqs.answer: We supply all major diamond shapes at the wholesale level: round brilliant, oval, cushion, pear, princess, emerald, asscher, marquise, radiant, and heart. We also accommodate custom cutting requests for brand-specific designs through our Antwerp manufacturing facility.

faqs.question: Are IGI certified lab grown diamonds good?
faqs.answer: IGI (International Gemological Institute) is the world's largest independent gemological laboratory and the single most common certification body for lab grown diamonds globally. IGI certifications are widely accepted by jewelers, retailers and brands in Europe, Asia, the Americas, and across the global trade. IGI lab grown diamond grading reports confirm the 4Cs and are trusted by the wholesale and retail market. For most wholesale buyers of lab grown diamonds, IGI certification is the standard — and Uniglo Diamonds supplies IGI certified stones as part of our core inventory.

faqs.question: What is the difference between wholesale and retail pricing for lab grown diamonds?
faqs.answer: Retail pricing includes the margin of the retailer or jeweler selling to the end consumer. Wholesale pricing is the price paid before retail markup — it is the price at which a manufacturer or bulk supplier sells to trade buyers. By buying wholesale directly from Uniglo Diamonds, you access the price tier below retail — which is the foundation of your own margin when selling to consumers. The exact differential between wholesale and retail varies by stone, grade, and market, but wholesale buyers typically operate on margins of 30–100% depending on the retail channel and stone specification.

faqs.question: How do I verify a lab grown diamond is what it claims to be?
faqs.answer: The most reliable verification is the grading certificate. Match the certificate number to the diamond — most certified diamonds have the certificate number laser-inscribed on the girdle of the stone, visible under magnification. This allows any jeweler or buyer to confirm independently that the diamond in their hand matches the certificate on file. Beyond certification, specialist equipment such as a HPHT/CVD identification device can distinguish lab grown diamonds from natural diamonds and confirm the manufacturing method — though for commercial trade purposes, a valid GIA, IGI, or HRD certificate is the accepted standard.

faqs.question: Can I visit Uniglo Diamonds in Antwerp to view diamonds before purchasing?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our showroom is located at Hoveniersstraat 30, Suite 662/Bus 250, 2018 Antwerp — in the heart of Antwerp's diamond district. We welcome wholesale buyers, brand representatives, and private clients by appointment. Contact us to arrange a visit and we will prepare a selection of certified lab grown diamonds matched to your requirements.

faqs.question: Does Uniglo supply both natural and lab grown diamonds wholesale?
faqs.answer: Yes. Uniglo Diamonds is a full-spectrum diamond wholesale supplier. We supply both certified natural diamonds and certified lab grown diamonds, in loose and manufactured form, to wholesale and private buyers. This makes us particularly valuable to jewelers and brands who carry both product lines and want a single, trusted Antwerp supplier for their entire diamond inventory.

SourceLabGrownDiamondsPage.h1: How to Source Lab Grown Diamonds for Your Jewelry Business — The Complete Supplier Guide
SourceLabGrownDiamondsPage.faqSectionTitle: Frequently Asked Questions About Sourcing Lab Grown Diamonds for a Jewelry Business

content.text: To source lab grown diamonds for your jewelry business, work directly with a certified manufacturer rather than through brokers or trading platforms. The most reliable approach is to establish a direct supply relationship with an Antwerp-based diamond manufacturer — like Uniglo Diamonds — who can provide IGI, GIA, and HRD certified loose lab grown diamonds at wholesale pricing, with consistent quality, flexible quantities, and full supply chain documentation.

Sourcing lab grown diamonds for a jewelry business is one of the most important decisions you will make as a jeweler, designer, or retailer. Get it right and you build a supply chain that gives you better margins, better product quality, and a competitive advantage that is extremely difficult for competitors to replicate. Get it wrong — by sourcing from unreliable suppliers, unverified intermediaries, or uncertified stock — and you risk your reputation, your margins, and your customer relationships simultaneously.

This guide covers the entire sourcing process from start to finish. We explain what certified lab grown diamonds actually are, how the supply chain works, what to look for and what to avoid in a supplier, how pricing works at the wholesale level, and why working directly with an established Antwerp manufacturer is the single most commercially intelligent sourcing decision available to jewelry businesses in Europe and globally today.

article.heading: Why Lab Grown Diamonds Are Now a Business Necessity, Not a Choice

content.text: Five years ago, a jeweler could choose whether or not to stock lab grown diamonds. Today, that choice has effectively been made by the market. The global lab grown diamond market is projected to surpass $35 billion by 2030, growing at over 8% CAGR — far outpacing natural diamond demand.

The engagement ring market tells the story most clearly. In 2019, lab grown diamonds accounted for approximately 6% of engagement ring center stones. By 2025, that figure exceeded 47%. In less than six years, lab grown diamonds went from a niche alternative to the dominant choice for one of jewelry retail's highest-value product categories.

Retailers who shift 30–60% of their collection to lab grown diamonds consistently report one thing: profit margins increase — sometimes by 2x to 5x. This is not a promotional claim from a supplier — it is a commercial reality that follows directly from the price differential between lab grown and natural diamonds at the wholesale level, combined with consumers' increasing willingness to pay premium prices for premium lab grown stones.
The jewelry businesses that are winning in 2025 and 2026 are those who:

items: Established direct supply relationships with certified lab grown diamond manufacturers early — giving them wholesale price access that late movers cannot easily replicate.
Built their collections around lab grown diamonds proactively — rather than reactively adding a small lab grown section after losing sales to competitors who already offer them.

Educated their customers about lab grown diamonds confidently — because they source from manufacturers who provide the documentation, certification, and product knowledge to support that conversation.

content.text: If your jewelry business has not yet built a structured lab grown diamond sourcing strategy, this guide gives you everything you need to do so correctly.

article.heading: Understanding the Lab Grown Diamond Supply Chain

content.text: Before you can source effectively, you need to understand how the lab grown diamond supply chain works — because where you enter that chain determines the price you pay, the quality you receive, and the reliability of your supply.
Stage 1: Diamond Growing

Lab grown diamonds begin at the growing stage — either CVD (Chemical Vapor Deposition) or HPHT (High-Pressure High-Temperature) production facilities. CVD replicates natural diamond formation deep within the earth using high heat and intense pressure to turn carbon into crystal, while HPHT involves placing a diamond seed in a sealed chamber filled with carbon-rich gas that is ionised into plasma, causing carbon atoms to deposit and grow layer by layer. The growing process takes between two and four weeks for most commercial stones, and longer for very large or specialty diamonds.
Growing facilities are concentrated in a small number of global locations — India (particularly Surat), China, and increasingly, specialist operations in Europe and the United States.
Stage 2: Cutting and Polishing

A rough lab grown diamond crystal emerging from the growing stage is not the brilliant, faceted stone you see in jewelry. It goes through a separate cutting and polishing process — the same skilled craft that has been applied to natural diamonds for centuries. The quality of cutting directly affects the final diamond's brilliance, fire, and overall grade. A poorly cut lab grown diamond with excellent rough quality will underperform a well-cut stone with slightly lower rough quality. This is why the cutting stage — and where it happens — matters enormously to the final product you source as a jeweler.

Antwerp has been the world centre of diamond cutting and polishing for over five centuries. The skills, quality standards, and equipment concentrated in Antwerp's diamond district are unmatched globally. Diamonds cut and polished in Antwerp — whether natural or lab grown — carry an implicit quality standard that is recognised across every major jewelry market in the world.
Stage 3: Grading and Certification

After cutting and polishing, a finished lab grown diamond is submitted to an independent gemological laboratory for grading. The laboratory assesses and documents the diamond's four key quality parameters — Cut, Colour, Clarity, and Carat weight — and issues a grading report (certificate) that serves as the diamond's official quality record.

The three laboratories whose certificates are accepted as the global standard in the wholesale and retail jewelry market are GIA (Gemological Institute of America), IGI (International Gemological Institute), and HRD (Hoge Raad voor Diamant, based in Antwerp). Every certified lab grown diamond you source should carry a current report from one of these three institutions.
Stage 4: Wholesale Distribution

Certified finished diamonds enter the wholesale market — either through direct manufacturer relationships, trading platforms, or broker networks. This is the stage at which most jewelers and retailers source their inventory. The key variable is how many intermediaries exist between the manufacturer and your purchase order — because every intermediary adds margin to the price you ultimately pay.
Stage 5: Retail
The diamond reaches the end consumer through your business — either mounted in a jewelry piece you manufacture or design, or as a loose certified stone for a client commissioning a bespoke setting.

The critical insight for jewelers: The closer you can get to Stage 3 in this supply chain — buying directly from a certified manufacturer rather than through multiple intermediary stages — the better your pricing, the more reliable your quality, and the stronger your supply relationship. Uniglo Diamonds operates at Stage 3 — we are the manufacturer and the wholesale supplier in one.

article.heading: What to Look for in a Lab Grown Diamond Supplier

content.text: Not every lab grown diamond supplier is the right partner for your jewelry business. Here is the framework you should apply when evaluating any supplier — including us.
Certification: Non-Negotiable Baseline

Every diamond in every order must carry a current grading report from GIA, IGI, or HRD. No exceptions. A supplier who cannot provide certified stones — or who offers uncertified stones "at a better price" — is not a supplier worth working with. Certification is not paperwork. It is the independent verification that the grade assigned to the diamond is accurate, consistent, and internationally recognised. Without it, you cannot make accurate claims to your retail customers, you cannot price your product correctly, and you have no recourse if the quality does not match what was described.
Manufacturing Origin: Know Where the Diamond Was Made

Ask every prospective supplier directly: are these diamonds grown in your own facility, or do you source from third parties? The answer has major implications for quality control, supply consistency, and your ability to make meaningful claims about the diamond's origin to your customers. A direct manufacturer has full visibility and accountability across the entire production process. A reseller has none.
Supply Consistency: Can They Deliver What You Need, When You Need It?

The best supplier relationship is one where you can reliably forecast your inventory. Ask about lead times for standard orders, availability across the shapes and grades you sell most, and what happens if a specific stone you need is temporarily unavailable. A quality supplier should have a broad, well-managed inventory and the flexibility to accommodate your ordering patterns over time.
Flexibility on Specifications: Shapes, Grades, and Sizes

Your jewelry business has specific requirements — particular shapes that sell in your market, clarity and colour grades that match your price point, carat ranges that your customers consistently request. A strong lab grown diamond supplier should be able to accommodate the full range of shapes (round brilliant, oval, cushion, pear, princess, emerald, asscher, marquise, radiant, heart), a broad range of carat weights, and the full spectrum of colour and clarity grades. If a supplier can only offer a narrow range of specifications, your collection flexibility will be permanently constrained by their limitations.
Pricing Transparency: Clear, Documented, and Consistent

Wholesale pricing should be transparent and documentable. Your supplier should provide written pricing — either as a price list or as quoted pricing on specific stones — that you can use for your own margin planning and customer pricing. Prices that are given verbally and change between conversations are a red flag. A professional wholesale supplier prices consistently, documents pricing in writing, and gives adequate notice of any market-driven pricing adjustments.
Track Record and Industry Standing

How long has this supplier been operating? Do they have verifiable standing in the industry — certifications, memberships, industry references? A supplier with decades of experience in the diamond industry, operating from a recognised diamond centre like Antwerp, brings a depth of expertise and accountability that newer entrants simply cannot replicate.

article.heading: The Difference Between Sourcing from Antwerp vs Other Markets

content.text: For European jewelers and brands — and indeed for global buyers seeking the highest standards — Antwerp remains the world's preeminent diamond sourcing location. Understanding why matters for your sourcing decisions.

Antwerp processes approximately 84% of the world's rough diamonds annually. The city's diamond district — centred on Hoveniersstraat, where Uniglo Diamonds has been located for over 30 years — contains the highest concentration of diamond cutting expertise, grading infrastructure, and established trade relationships anywhere on Earth.

The quality standards applied in Antwerp are enforced through decades of competitive practice and institutional oversight. HRD — one of the three globally recognised diamond grading authorities — is headquartered in Antwerp. The Antwerp World Diamond Centre (AWDC) operates regulatory and support frameworks for the entire Antwerp diamond trade. The city is a member-in-good-standing of the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme, and its lab grown diamond operations are subject to the same rigorous standards applied to natural diamonds.

For jewelers sourcing lab grown diamonds, buying from an established Antwerp manufacturer means buying from the city that has set the global standard in diamond quality for five centuries. That heritage does not automatically guarantee quality from every Antwerp-based supplier — but it does mean the institutional framework for quality assurance and accountability is stronger in Antwerp than in most alternative sourcing markets.
Antwerp vs Online Trading Platforms

items: On a trading platform, you typically do not know the actual manufacturer of the diamonds you are purchasing.
Quality control standards vary significantly between the different sellers operating on the same platform.
Pricing includes platform fees and seller margins stacked on top of the underlying wholesale price.

Post-purchase support — if a stone is not as described, or if a quality issue arises — is limited by the platform's dispute resolution process rather than by a direct relationship with the manufacturer.

content.text: A direct manufacturer relationship like the one Uniglo Diamonds offers to wholesale buyers provides none of these limitations. You know exactly who made the diamond, where it was made, how it was graded, and who is accountable for the quality of every stone in every order.

article.heading: How Wholesale Pricing Works for Lab Grown Diamonds

content.text: Understanding lab grown diamond pricing helps you make better sourcing decisions and build more accurate margin models for your jewelry business.

Lab grown diamonds cost significantly less to produce than mined diamonds due to advanced CVD and HPHT technology, and that cost advantage flows through the supply chain. At the retail level, a 1-carat lab grown diamond typically sells for approximately $800–$1,200 — compared to $3,000–$6,000 for an equivalent natural diamond. At the wholesale level, the price differential is even more pronounced, because the wholesale margin on lab grown diamonds is typically higher in percentage terms than on natural diamonds.

Retailers enjoy higher profit margins of 30–60% more than mined diamonds when selling lab grown stones — a direct result of the combination of lower wholesale cost and strong consumer willingness to pay premium prices for high-quality certified lab grown diamonds.
Several factors determine the wholesale price of a specific lab grown diamond:

items: Carat weight is the primary price driver. Larger stones command disproportionately higher prices — the relationship between carat weight and price is exponential, not linear. A 2-carat stone does not cost twice what a 1-carat stone costs; it typically costs three to five times more, depending on grade.

Colour grade significantly affects price. The GIA and IGI colour scale runs from D (colourless) to Z (light yellow). Colourless stones (D-F) command a significant premium over near-colourless (G-J) or faint yellow (K-M) grades. For most jewelry applications, G-H colour lab grown diamonds offer the best balance of visual quality and price efficiency.

Clarity grade similarly affects price. Internally Flawless (IF) and Very Very Slightly Included (VVS1/VVS2) stones command premiums over Very Slightly Included (VS1/VS2) and Slightly Included (SI1/SI2) grades. For most jewelry settings, VS2 or SI1 clarity is eye-clean and offers excellent value at the wholesale level.

Cut grade affects both price and the stone's visual performance. For round brilliant lab grown diamonds, Excellent cut is the benchmark. For fancy shapes (oval, cushion, pear, etc.), cut assessment is more nuanced and certified grading reports provide detailed cut information.

Manufacturing method (CVD vs HPHT) can affect pricing at the margin, with CVD colorless diamonds sometimes commanding a small premium in the wholesale market due to their reputation for exceptional optical purity.

content.text: At Uniglo Diamonds, we provide transparent wholesale pricing on all specifications — documented, consistent, and based on current market rates. Contact our wholesale team for current pricing on the specific shapes, grades, and carat weights relevant to your business.

article.heading: Building Your Lab Grown Diamond Sourcing Strategy

content.text: Effective sourcing is not a one-time transaction — it is a strategy you build and refine over time. Here is how to approach building a sustainable lab grown diamond supply chain for your jewelry business.
Step 1: Define Your Core Specification Range

Before approaching any supplier, define clearly what your business actually needs. What shapes do your customers request most? What carat ranges sell in your price point? What colour and clarity grades align with your product positioning? Having a defined specification range makes the sourcing conversation more efficient and allows your supplier to maintain appropriate stock for your specific needs.
Step 2: Prioritise Certification From the Start

Decide which certification or certifications your business will require on all sourced stones. GIA, IGI, and HRD are all internationally recognised, but different market segments may have preferences. European consumers increasingly recognise IGI lab grown diamond certificates. High-end markets may prefer GIA. If you operate across markets, a supplier like Uniglo Diamonds who can provide all three certifications gives you maximum flexibility.
Step 3: Start With a Trial Order

Before committing to a full supply relationship, request a trial order of a representative selection of stones. Evaluate the quality against the grading reports. Assess the packaging, documentation, and delivery experience. A professional wholesale supplier will welcome a trial order because they are confident in what they supply.
Step 4: Build the Relationship, Not Just the Transaction

The best wholesale supplier relationships are long-term partnerships, not spot purchases. A supplier who knows your business, your specifications, and your ordering patterns will serve you better over time — maintaining appropriate stock for your needs, alerting you to exceptional stones that match your requirements, and working with you on pricing as your volume grows.
Step 5: Document Everything

Maintain complete documentation for every diamond you source — grading reports, invoices, supplier certification documentation, and supply chain records. This is important for compliance in regulated markets, for insurance purposes, and increasingly for customer transparency expectations. European consumers in particular are asking more detailed questions about diamond provenance — your ability to answer those questions confidently starts with your supplier documentation.

article.heading: Why Uniglo Diamonds Is the Right Sourcing Partner for Your Jewelry Business

content.text: Jewelers and brands across Europe choose Uniglo Diamonds as their lab grown diamond supplier for consistent reasons. Here is what distinguishes our wholesale supply relationship from the alternatives available to you.

items: Direct manufacturer access in Antwerp. You are not buying from a broker, a trading platform, or an importer. That means true wholesale pricing, direct quality accountability, and a supply relationship built on decades of manufacturing expertise.

Full shape and specification range. We maintain inventory across all major shapes — round brilliant, oval, cushion, pear, princess, emerald, asscher, marquise, radiant, and heart — and across a broad range of carat weights, colour grades, and clarity grades. We also accommodate custom cutting requests through our Antwerp manufacturing facility for brand-specific design requirements.

Whether you are a small independent jeweler placing your first lab grown diamond order, or an established retail chain making large volume purchases, we work with your actual business requirements. We do not impose minimum order quantities that do not reflect the reality of your business size and growth stage.

Diamond financing for inventory investment. Building a lab grown diamond inventory requires capital. Uniglo Diamonds offers flexible financing options for qualified wholesale buyers — allowing you to build the inventory your business needs without the full upfront capital requirement. This is particularly valuable for growing jewelry businesses managing the balance between inventory investment and operational cash flow.

Free diamond estimation service. If you carry existing diamond inventory — natural or lab grown — that you are evaluating for trade, resale, or insurance purposes, Uniglo Diamonds provides a complimentary, no-obligation estimation service. This adds practical value to the broader business relationship beyond the supply function.

article.heading: The Market Opportunity for Jewelers Who Source Lab Grown Diamonds Now

content.text: The window for establishing a strong position in the lab grown diamond market is still open — but the competitive dynamics are shifting quickly. The global lab grown diamond market is expected to grow from $29.73 billion in 2024 to $97.85 billion by 2034 — a market that will nearly triple in size over the next decade. The jewelry businesses building their lab grown supply chains now, with established certified suppliers, are the ones who will be positioned to serve that growing market most effectively.

With millennials and Gen Z prioritizing sustainability, transparency, and affordability, lab grown diamonds have become the cornerstone of modern luxury jewelry. These demographic groups represent the primary engagement ring and fine jewelry purchasing cohort for the next 15 years. Building your supply chain and product offering around lab grown diamonds now is not a trend-chasing decision — it is a long-term strategic alignment with where your most valuable customers are already heading.

faq.question: How do I start sourcing lab grown diamonds from a manufacturer in Antwerp?
faq.answer: Contact Uniglo Diamonds directly through our website, by email at suraj@uniglodiamonds.com, or by phone at +32 03 500 91 07. We will discuss your business requirements, the shapes and grades you need, your ordering frequency, and your certification preferences. We can arrange a visit to our Antwerp showroom if you would like to view stones in person before establishing a supply relationship. The process is straightforward and does not involve lengthy registration processes or minimum commitment requirements.

faq.question: What certifications should lab grown diamonds have for retail sale in Europe?
faq.answer: In the European market, IGI and GIA certifications are both widely accepted and recognised by consumers and jewelers. HRD certification carries particular weight in the Belgian, Dutch, and broader European market due to HRD's Antwerp base. For most European retail applications, IGI certified lab grown diamonds are the most commonly supplied and recognised standard. If you sell to international markets, GIA certification may be preferred by certain high-end segments. Uniglo Diamonds can supply any of the three certifications depending on your requirements.

faq.question: How long does it take to receive an order of wholesale lab grown diamonds?
faq.answer: Delivery timelines depend on whether the stones you require are in our current inventory or require specific cutting and grading. Stones available from existing certified inventory are typically delivered within a few business days to European destinations. Custom orders requiring specific cutting or grading may take three to five weeks depending on complexity and volume. We provide specific timeline information at the point of order placement.

faq.question: Can I visit the Uniglo Diamonds facility in Antwerp before placing a wholesale order?
faq.answer: Yes — and we actively encourage this for new wholesale accounts. Seeing the stones in person, under proper lighting and magnification, is the best way to assess quality before committing to a supply relationship. Our facility is located at Hoveniersstraat 30, Suite 662/Bus 250, 2018 Antwerp. Contact us to arrange an appointment and we will prepare a selection of certified lab grown diamonds matching your specifications.

faq.question: What is the difference between IGI and GIA certification for lab grown diamonds?
faq.answer: Both GIA and IGI are internationally recognised independent gemological laboratories. IGI is the world's largest independent gemological laboratory and is the most widely used certification body for lab grown diamonds globally. IGI has dedicated lab grown diamond grading infrastructure and its certificates are accepted across Europe, Asia, and the Americas. GIA is the most globally prestigious grading institution overall and its lab grown diamond reports carry particular weight in the US high-end market and with certain luxury jewelry brands. For most European wholesale buyers of lab grown diamonds, IGI certification is the standard. The choice between them depends on your specific market and customer base — Uniglo Diamonds can supply either.

faq.question: How does lab grown diamond sourcing affect my profit margins compared to natural diamonds?
faq.answer: Most retailers don't realise that the biggest margin booster is not just lab grown diamonds — it's the source you buy from. Buying direct from a manufacturer eliminates middlemen and reduces cost per carat, while perfect calibration for matched sets and consistent supply of popular shapes allows retailers to offer competitive prices while still enjoying healthy profit margins. As a practical guide, jewelers sourcing lab grown diamonds directly from a manufacturer like Uniglo Diamonds typically achieve retail margins of 40–80% on lab grown stones — significantly higher than the margins achievable on equivalent natural diamond inventory.

faq.question: Do lab grown diamonds come with a laser inscription on the girdle?
faq.answer: Most certified lab grown diamonds have the grading report number laser-inscribed on the girdle of the stone — visible under 10x magnification. This inscription allows the grading report to be matched definitively to the specific stone it describes, providing both buyer and seller with confirmed traceability. At Uniglo Diamonds, we confirm inscription status for every stone at the point of order.

faq.question: Can Uniglo Diamonds supply matched parcels for jewelry manufacturing?
faq.answer: Yes. Matched parcels — sets of diamonds with consistent colour, clarity, and cut grades for use in multi-stone jewelry pieces such as tennis bracelets, eternity rings, or pavé settings — are a standard part of our wholesale supply capability. Our manufacturing facility in Antwerp has the capacity to produce matched sets to precise specifications for jewelry manufacturing applications. Contact our wholesale team with your parcel requirements and we will advise on availability and pricing.

faq.question: What documentation do I receive with a wholesale order from Uniglo Diamonds?
faq.answer: Every order includes the grading report(s) for each certified stone, a detailed invoice with full stone specifications, supply chain documentation confirming lab grown origin, and packaging appropriate for the value and quantity of stones ordered. For wholesale accounts requiring additional documentation for compliance, import/export, or insurance purposes, we accommodate those requirements on a case-by-case basis.

faq.question: Is it possible to source both natural and lab grown diamonds from Uniglo Diamonds?
faq.answer: Yes. Uniglo Diamonds is a full-spectrum wholesale diamond supplier. We supply both certified natural diamonds and certified lab grown diamonds across all major shapes and grades. For jewelry businesses who stock both product lines, working with a single established Antwerp supplier for your entire diamond inventory simplifies sourcing, documentation, and relationship management considerably.
